bleeding: Apply firm direct pressure with a clean cloth or bandage.
Keep pressure steady until trained responders arrive.
If possible, keep the injured area elevated above heart level.
fracture: Do not move the injured limb unless the scene is unsafe.
Immobilize the area with padding or a splint if trained.
Apply a cold pack wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling.
cardiac arrest: Start hands-only CPR: push hard and fast in the center of the chest.
Ask someone to bring the nearest AED immediately.
chest pain: Keep the person seated and calm.
Loosen tight clothing and monitor breathing.
Do not give food or drink. Prepare for CPR if they become unresponsive.
burns: Cool the burn with clean running water for at least 20 minutes.
Remove jewelry or tight clothing near the burn if not stuck to skin.
Cover with a clean, non-stick dressing. Do not apply ice.
unknown: Make sure the scene is safe before approaching.
Keep the person still and monitor breathing.
Follow dispatcher or LifeLine AI guidance until help arrives.
